ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya di teesi, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti sa'am Musa zaaligu ni anabidima la bulisigu yɛla. Ay! N bu kyɛ na ni n ti sa'am a ni, amaa ni n ti kyɛ ka a niŋi yɛlimaŋni.
translated: And Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.” Oh! If I were to say it to you, but I am not the one to say it to you.

text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn suuri yisigi n‑niŋi u mabiiya, u bɛ la sariya karibu ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa!’, ba nin boosi u boosa gyintooriba duu maa ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin gyɛrigu wa!’, u nin di wahala bugum ni.
translated: If a person is sitting somewhere and someone comes and greets his children, he will be happy and say, "You have respected me!" and he will not take the person as an enemy. But if someone comes and insults his children, he will be angry with that person.

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di zabi ni ya ta'ai ya taalli daana tuma; faasee ka sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u.
translated: If you want to go, then you must decide and prepare yourself well; because if you just follow others blindly, you may end up in trouble.

text: I yi ya puusi Naawunni, di niŋi hoo birikɔnsi dima: ba boori ba zɛ saazugu m‑puusiri gyeeŋŋu duri ni, ni sɔkyira ni, ni niriba ti nya ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.
translated: If you see a Naawunni cat, you must be very careful: do not touch its tail or its ears, or its whiskers, or its eyes. People say that if you do, you will have bad luck all year.

translated: If someone does not have enough money, God will provide for them so that they and their family will have enough.

text: Ni, “Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi daabri n‑ti dugmadima ayi: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka laa n‑zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.”

translated: He called the children and said, “Who among you can go to the forest and bring me some firewood and then come and tell me?”

text: Bee, ya teesim daam yɛla: ba mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akura ni. Ba yi niŋi lala, ga'akura maa nin ŋma, ka daam maa yuuli m‑basi, ka ga'a maa gba sa'am. Ay! Ga'apaala ka ba zaŋŋni n leeri dapaalli n‑niŋni a ni, lala ka abayi maa zaa nin kpalim suŋŋa.”

text: Ni, “Ya di teesi, ni, m mari la nangbanyinni beesigu n‑kyɛ duniya wa na. Ay! M bu kyɛ na ni m mari nangbanyinni yɛla na, see zaba.
translated: He said, "I am suffering, I have never experienced such hardship in this world. Oh! If I had known that I would face such hardship, I would not have come."

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.

translated: If you see someone’s problem and you don’t help, and a poor person and you don’t give, and a child and you don’t show them the right way, you are not good.

translated: If you always say, "Everyone is bad except me," and everyone is bad except you, then God is also bad, because God is among everyone.

text: Di saha ka Gyɔni daa bɛ dansarika ni - Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa su niriba Naawunni koom la. U daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni seem, ka daa tum u nyaandɔliba Yisa saani na,

translated: What if someone comes and asks you about it? What will you say? - The elders have already spoken and there is nothing more to add.

text: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, ‘Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑lɔ zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi daandima.’ Amaa Naawunni yam tumni n‑wulisiri kamaŋŋa yɛla.”
translated: If someone from Ninsaala Bii comes and someone meets people sitting and talking, and says, ‘Why are these people always sitting here talking and laughing? – and he does not know that they are elders and wise people discussing important matters.’ But Naawunni knows how to listen and understand well.

text: Bee, ya na zi n‑karim Musa zaaligu yɛla? Ni, maalimaanniba tumni la ba tuma Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni Vuusim Dabsiri. Ban n tumni la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ka bu deaari taalli di zugu.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Sɔppu la yɛliya, ni, ‘Man Naawunni bu boori ni ya maanni m‑maanni bunkɔbri wa, amaa m boori ya zɔri niriba namboo.’ Ya siri da mi dinŋɔ gbinni, ya naa n‑ku galim niriba ka ba bu niŋi taalli halli beela.
translated: God said, ‘If I find fifty righteous people in the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.’ Abraham answered, saying, he was only dust and ashes, and dared to speak to God.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.

translated: If a person wants to build a house and does not have money or strength, he or she must ask the Ninsaala Bii for help so that they can get the money or strength they need.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, ka kur kyɛ ka ninvugsuma dabɔya veela,

text: Dinzugu ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, halli ka ya ti yɛli ‘Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na!’ ”

text: Dinŋɔ zugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla maa, lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa ti ya kyɛ na.”

text: Ay! - Tanni maa daa tumni ka maŋtoori tuma n‑kyɛ ka kyi maa ti niŋi: a daa daŋŋi m‑buli m‑pusi n‑dɔ'ai, halli nti piri.
translated: Hey! - That is why you should not do such things and say: you should not eat dog or cat meat, and also pig.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Ka ka daa buli, n‑zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaasa, ka daa tɔ wula, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri a zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.”
translated: If you wake up early, you will see the morning star shining brightly, and if you delay, you will not see it because the sun will have already risen.

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Ka dabeem pam daa maali n‑gbaai ba, ka ba daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba, ni, “Bɔ nira nŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asiri pɛbsigu ni mɔ'ari gba ka a wumna!”
translated: If someone comes to you and asks for advice, and says, “What should I do? – but he already has his own idea and is only asking for advice to confirm what he wants to do!”

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Halli ba daa pun zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba, amaa u daa kur kyisiri a ni - sɔsɔ daa ka yaa n nin toaai n‑gbaai u suŋŋa.
translated: If someone takes something from you by force, do not fight with them, but rather let them have it - always do what is good and do not repay evil for evil.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Lala zugu u daa kpalim m‑bɛ gbala maa ni, ni zɔya zugu, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa. U daa kaasira, ka mari kuga n‑ŋmaari u maŋŋa.
translated: When night comes, everyone goes to their homes, and the town becomes quiet and empty. It is peaceful, and there is no noise to disturb anyone.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Tɔ; doo wa daa pun bɛ wa'aligu n‑nya Yisa,
translated: So; that person will never see Jesus’ face,

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Ka u daa ti ba soori, ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni - kurikyuudima maa mun daa paai tusa ayi - ka ba daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom daa di ba.
translated: If you do not have patience, you will not get good results – good results do not just come easily – you will not succeed and people will not respect you and life will not go well for you.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka ziim bu goaara.
translated: If someone wants to be truly wise, they must listen carefully and learn many things, and not speak without thinking.

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa na gɔsiri n‑gyinna, ni u ti nya ŋɔn siisi u.
translated: If someone does not stand in the rain, they will not know how cold it is.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Di saha ka pɔ'a maa daa gyigri ni dabeem ka mun mi din niŋi u seem, ka u daa kyɛ na nti gbaani Yisa tooni ka yɛli u din niŋi u maa zaa kasi.
translated: When a child is born and the parents want to give him a name, they usually wait until after seven days before they call his name in front of everyone.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.
translated: Every day, the wise person reflects on these three things: - they do not do much work or have many children. Also, they do not associate with troublemakers or lazy people at all.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Ka u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, “N nin ti i nyin boori seelli kam zaa halli di yi nyɛ n naam tingbaŋŋu maa purigri.”
translated: And he looked at them and said, “I do not see any reason why all of you should not enter my father’s house together.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.
translated: If they do not listen to your advice and do not follow your instructions, leave them.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Ka saha ŋɔn daa pun yuui ka yuŋŋu zibri maa, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, “Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni.
translated: If someone does something bad to you and your heart is troubled, the wise person will tell you, “Let go of the bad thing, and don’t keep it in your heart so it doesn’t harm you.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di.” Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i boori la ti kyaŋŋi nti da ligri pam paanu na n‑diisi ba, bee? Ay! - ti ligri ku paai lala!”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid, just believe and you will be saved.” But they replied to him, saying, “Master, have you not seen how many people have already died before you came, huh? Oh! - it is too late now!”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Di saha ka Yisa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba kyɛ ka niriba maa puri ba mansi n‑zinni mɔmaasa la zugu.
translated: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, "My child, your sins are forgiven."

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa, sahanseaa u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa, Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni la n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri sa n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.
translated: One day, as he was teaching the people, Jesus saw that there were many people and he asked his disciples to find a place where they could rest, and they went to a quiet place near Bethsaida.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.
translated: If someone does not respect his father, he will not find good things in life and will not receive God's blessings.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Ban daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni la, bɛni niriba maa daa baŋŋi ya'abi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u,
translated: If you do not forgive others, then your Father will not forgive you, either, says Jesus.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: “Yarima muna - ya za'asiri Naawunni soori, ni ya ti dɔli ya mammaŋŋa kaari maa: yam nla, bee?
translated: The prince said - he has finished Naawunni's work, and he has come to ask his mother for money: is that not so?

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya birikɔ'anu dima yɛla seem la; ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun yi n saani Ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;
translated: And Yisa’s father said to him, “The prophet Isaiah’s mouth truly had a message which he spoke long ago; and he said, listen, God says, ‘People will hear but not understand. They will look but not see, because their hearts have become hard; and their ears cannot hear.’

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: ka mɔŋŋi u ba ni u ma dizugu, ni, u ka taalli.
translated: If someone does not have patience, then they will fail.

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”
translated: If you do not understand this, you should ask your elders or those who know the word of God. And do not just keep quiet and let it pass you by.

ref: The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.”
translated: If someone does something bad to you and you forgive them, God will also forgive you. But if you do not forgive someone who does something bad to you, God will not forgive you.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”
translated: They say that every problem has a solution except death, so one should not lose hope.

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yisa daa yisigi n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya tiŋŋa la. U daa kpɛ yiri ni ka bu boori ni sɔsɔ baŋŋi, ni, u bɛ bɛni. Amaa u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai.
translated: God first created the world and placed the first man in a place called Taaya. He made a house for him and gave him seeds and animals, and he was happy. But he did not know how to farm.

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Mboo! Labsigu seaa nyin labsi wa zugu, kur labma n‑kuli; i nin paai n‑nya ka arizinni maa yiya, ka kyɛ i bipuŋŋa maa!”
translated: And Jesus said to him, "No! If you do not wash my feet, you have no part with me; you will not share in my inheritance, nor in my people!"

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa u kɔkɔ ka kyɛ zama maa, ka zaŋŋi u nubisi n‑tum doo maa tuba ni; ka tusi nantoori n‑zaŋŋi n‑siisi doo maa zulimni.
translated: And Jesus said that he does not have a place to lay his head, and that foxes have holes but the Son of Man does not; and birds of the air have nests but the Son of Man does not have anywhere to rest.
